text: Send Hermes with a message to another god
outcome: He grins. 'Which god?' You name one. He vanishes — and returns in three heartbeats with a reply. 'They said yes. Whatever you asked — they said yes.' He winks. 'I may have improved your wording slightly. You're welcome.'

text: Race Hermes across the keep walls
outcome: You run. He lets you almost win — you know he lets you — but for one moment, your fingers brush the finish line before his. He laughs, delighted. 'Fast for a mortal! Here.' He hands you a feather. 'Break this when you need me. I'll come. Once.'

text: Ask Hermes what secret he knows about you
outcome: He leans close, breath smelling of ozone and mischief. 'Your mother is not dead. She is in the underworld. Persephone knows exactly where. I was not supposed to tell you this — which is exactly why I am telling you.' He grins. 'You owe me.'

text: Hire Hermes to spy on your half-brother's court
outcome: He names a price — not gold, but a story. 'Tell me something true about yourself that you have never told anyone.' You do. He listens without grinning for once. 'Payment accepted.' He vanishes. Three days later, a scroll appears on your desk. Your half-brother's plans. Every one.
